column2: What are your general observations/impressions about the Batangas State University institutional visit?
column3: What AI tools/technology or facilities from the Batangas State University stood out as relevant to your office's work or the Academy's offerings?
column4: What potential activities, practices, or approaches from the visit can be adopted or adapted for our programs and offerings?
column5: What possible areas of collaboration with the Batangas State University do you see (e.g. training, research, joint projects, access to facilities)?
column9: Rate your overall experience of the Institutional Visit. (1-Poor, 5-Excellent)
column10: Any additional recommendations or ideas for the next steps?

column2: My overall impression of the institutional visit to Batangas State University is positive. The discussions showed the University's strong focus on research, innovation and collaboration.  VP Amante's team demonstrated clear direction of their programs, and the facilities presented during the visit reflected their readiness to support future partnerships. The visit offered useful insights into the institution's priorities and potential areas for cooperation.
column3: The AI tools and facilities that stood out were those focused on data analytics, machine learning, and research support. Their labs are useful for our work in digital initiatives and can complement the Academy's that introduce emerging technologies to public sector leaders.
column4: Their approach to showcasing applied research and technology-driven solutions can also be adapted to enrich our program delivery and enhance participants' practical learning experiences.
column5: Access to their specialized laboratories and facilities can support capacity-building initiatives and technical learning components of our programs.
column10: A recommended next step is to formalize the potential areas of collaboration through a follow-up meeting to identify specific activities and timelines. Establishing clear coordination points from both sides will help ensure smooth planning and implementation.

column2: My overall impression of the Batangas State University visit is very positive. I did not expect their advanced AI, data, and future technology initiatives. The visit showed that the university is forward-thinking, innovative, and well-prepared for emerging technologies.
column3: The AI tools and technology that stood out the most to me were those showcased at the STEER Building. The facilities demonstrated how advanced AI, data, and technology infrastructures can significantly enhance program delivery.  In relation to our office’s work and the Academy’s offerings, I believe DAP would greatly benefit from having a dedicated floor focused on AI, data, and emerging technologies. I also recommend that we invest further in upgrading and strengthening our IGH and related facilities to align with current technological advancements.
column4: We can adopt some of their internal practices, particularly in strengthening our data infrastructure, server setup, and internet system monitoring. These improvements can support more efficient operations and better program delivery.
column5: Possible areas of collaboration include joint projects or partnerships in research, training, and technology or AI-related initiatives, where both institutions can share expertise and resources.
column10: Thank you for the invitation. I look forward to another institutional visit and similar learning opportunities in the future.

column2: They possessed a clear and compelling vision for their future and took deliberate steps to realize it. This included substantial investments in emerging and future-ready technologies.
column3: The tools they use, along with the processes and metrics they follow in managing their ICT operations—from server management to software application development.
column4: Perhaps we can gather the geotags of our clients’ offices so we can visually map the geographic reach of our interventions.
column5: Possible areas of collaboration could include offering Technical Assistance on ICTD good practices, particularly in strengthening systems, processes, and digital governance capacities.
column10: Perhaps for the next institutional visit, a pamphlet can be provided containing a brief description of the facility, its core functions, key projects, and contact information.

column2: They have made significant investments in their IT manpower and infrastructure, which have benefited all units of the university. As a result, they are able to keep up with technological trends, establishing themselves as a premier national engineering university in the Philippines.
column3: The Digital Transformation Center plays a pivotal role in advancing the university’s technological capabilities, particularly through its work in artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning. These initiatives are not isolated; in fact, all of the university’s centers and laboratories integrate AI into their operations, each applying it in unique and innovative ways.
column4: I am particularly interested in their microcredential programs, which I believe could serve as an excellent model for us to adopt. Microcredentials have become increasingly significant in today’s educational landscape, offering learners targeted, skills-based certifications that complement traditional degrees. In fact, these short, specialized programs are now considered almost as valuable as a bachelor’s or graduate degree, especially in industries that prioritize practical expertise and continuous learning.
column10: Maybe, schedule a meeting dedicated to sharing insights and discussing how we can leverage the resources they currently have.

column2: The BatStateU demonstrates strong leadership in innovation and national development through its proactive AI initiatives, robust research infrastructure, and strategic industry-academe partnerships aligned with global best practices.
column3: N-CAIR and CAIST are most relevant to DAP for AI innovation and research, while the Digital Transformation Center and GIS Center support digital governance and data analytics; KIST Park offers a strong model for industry-academe collaboration to boost public sector innovation.
column4: The DAP can learn from BatStateU by setting up innovation hubs, adding AI and digital tools to training programs, working closely with agencies and schools, using local data for better policies, and encouraging a culture of innovation.
column5: The DAP can work with BatStateU on training programs, joint research, innovation projects, and using their AI and tech facilities.
column10: Partnership with BatStateU, piloting joint AI and innovation projects, organizing knowledge-sharing sessions, and exploring access to their research facilities for collaborative programs.

column2: BatStateU is widely recognized as a leading engineering university in the Philippines, with a strong focus on engineering, technology, and innovation. The institution emphasizes its research efforts and innovations, industry collaborations, and dedication to developing graduates who are competitive on a global scale.  Furthermore, they have continually invested in modern laboratories, technology hubs, and facilities.
column3: BatStateU has been actively developing Artificial Intelligence (AI)-related initiatives that align well with the goals of the Development Academy of the Philippines (DAP), especially in areas like capacity building.  BatStateU offers short courses, certifications, or graduate programs in AI which can complement DAP’s capacity-building initiatives.  Additionally, BatStateU utilizes open-source technologies and maintains an in-house development team to ensure cost efficiency, enhance flexibility, and reduce dependency on external developers.
column4: Since the Philippines’ comprehensive AI legislation is still in progress alongside the development of its National AI Plan, the DAP can consider benchmarking its framework against Singapore's National AI Strategy 2.0, similar to what BatStateU has done.   Additionally, the DAP may request BatStateU to share the open-source applications they use, which the DAP could also adopt.
column5: If the DAP conducts training on AI, they can include a site visit to BatStateU as part of their program of activities to showcase the university’s AI-related initiatives and best practices. In cases where there is insufficient space or facility limitations, DAP may opt to rent venues or innovation hubs within BatStateU to accommodate participants. The DAP can also engage BatStateU to produce plaques and other printed materials.  Lastly, for our unit, the AO25 Secretariat may explore collaboration with BatStateU to assist in developing a dashboard that tracks the performance of agencies under the PBB from 2012 to 2023.

column4: Use of Open Source program for IT projects

column2: Leadership/management support played a vital role in making these initiatives and innovations successful and sustainable
column3: Simulation System,  SIGAW and LIKHA Fab Lab
column4: Assistance/extension services to LGUs or communities, microcredentialing
column5: technical training and access to facilities
